evidence_positioning: Owlnest Lume is an evidence-informed compact circadian rhythm lamp. Published research and institutional references on the Science page support the underlying lighting principles. Product-specific clinical outcomes have not been claimed.
claim_boundary: The research sources support design principles only. They do not mean Owlnest Lume has completed product-specific clinical trials or can diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent disease.

evidence_levels.name: Published Research
evidence_levels.summary: Published studies, institutional research, technical reports, and research summaries support the principle that evening light spectrum, blue-light exposure, and nighttime lighting environments can affect wind-down behavior and natural melatonin rhythm.

evidence_levels.name: Design Translation
evidence_levels.summary: Owlnest Lume translates these principles into a compact evening lamp with a deep amber / red-orange dusk-like glow, blue-light-minimized spectrum direction, use timing 1 to 2 hours before bed, and a low-stimulation nighttime lighting environment.

evidence_levels.name: Product-Specific Validation
evidence_levels.summary: Product-specific validation should include spectral power distribution, CCT measurement, illuminance at common bedside distances, blue-light or melanopic data if available, and testing context. Current status: pending.

research_sources.year_if_available: Year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.author_or_institution_if_available: Author/year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.key_idea: Red-spectrum nighttime light strategies are discussed in relation to sleep quality and recovery-oriented behavior.
research_sources.relevance_to_owlnest_lume: Supports the broader design direction toward red-orange nighttime light rather than bright white evening lighting.
research_sources.claim_boundary: Supports a design principle, but does not prove product-specific clinical outcomes for Owlnest Lume.

research_sources.source_title: CIE S 026: Measuring Biologically Relevant Light
research_sources.year_if_available: Year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.key_idea: Provides a framework for describing biologically relevant light, not just visible brightness.
research_sources.relevance_to_owlnest_lume: Supports the design logic that nighttime lighting should be discussed in terms of biological relevance, spectrum direction, and timing.
research_sources.claim_boundary: This is a measurement framework, not proof that Owlnest Lume has completed product-specific spectral validation.

research_sources.source_title: How Light Spectrum Regulates Human Melatonin
research_sources.year_if_available: Year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.author_or_institution_if_available: Author/year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.key_idea: Human melatonin timing is sensitive to light spectrum, not only to total brightness.
research_sources.relevance_to_owlnest_lume: Supports the emphasis on spectrum direction in a nighttime lamp rather than treating all warm light as equivalent.
research_sources.claim_boundary: Supports the underlying lighting principle, but does not show that Owlnest Lume itself changes melatonin in a measured trial.

research_sources.source_title: How Evening Room Light Suppresses Melatonin
research_sources.year_if_available: Year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.author_or_institution_if_available: Author/year not clearly available from current Science page
research_sources.key_idea: Ordinary room light in the evening can affect melatonin timing and shorten the body's nighttime signal.
research_sources.relevance_to_owlnest_lume: Supports the product's positioning around using less stimulating nighttime light instead of harsh bright room light before bed.
research_sources.claim_boundary: Supports why evening light choices matter, but does not prove product-specific outcomes for Owlnest Lume.

research_sources.claim_boundary: This supports design framing only and does not establish product-specific clinical efficacy for Owlnest Lume.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: How Light Spectrum Regulates Human Melatonin
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Evening light spectrum matters, not only brightness.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Use a deep amber / red-orange nighttime spectrum direction.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Deep amber dusk-like glow, not white or pale yellow.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: How Evening Room Light Suppresses Melatonin
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Blue-rich or stimulating room light can be more disruptive at night.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Minimize blue-light exposure during evening use.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Needs product-specific spectral measurement for quantitative claims.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: CIE S 026: Measuring Biologically Relevant Light
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Biologically relevant light should be measured with more than visible brightness.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Frame nighttime design around biological relevance and timing.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Circadian rhythm lamp positioning for nighttime use.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Not proof that Owlnest Lume has completed the same measurements.
design_traceability_matrix.validation_still_needed: Melanopic or equivalent product-specific metrics if available.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: Read: Smart Sleep Technology by ITRI
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Timing matters for circadian-friendly lighting environments.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Recommend use 1 to 2 hours before bed.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Positioned as an evening wind-down lamp, not daytime task lighting.
design_traceability_matrix.validation_still_needed: User study or controlled product-specific test if stronger claims are desired.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: Biologically Relevant Lighting: An Industry Perspective
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Lighting can be designed with biological effects in mind.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Position the product as evidence-informed circadian lighting.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Compact circadian rhythm lamp for evening wind-down and nighttime living.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Does not prove product-specific clinical efficacy.

design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Warm, candlelight-style spectrum directions can reduce blue-heavy nighttime light characteristics.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Favor a deeper amber / red-orange dusk-like light signature.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Night-friendly glow instead of ordinary warm lamp output.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Not a claim that Owlnest Lume has identical spectral performance.

design_traceability_matrix.science_page_source: High-Efficiency Candlelight OLED with Very Low Color Temperature
design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Very low color temperature can be intentionally engineered.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Use a low-stimulation spectrum direction for nighttime living.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Rich deep amber / red-orange dusk-like glow.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Not a quantitative performance claim for Owlnest Lume.

design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Candlelight-style light can be deliberately designed as a distinct lighting direction.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Nighttime transition tool with candlelight-adjacent visual intent.
design_traceability_matrix.claim_boundary: Supports direction and appearance only, not product-specific efficacy.
design_traceability_matrix.validation_still_needed: Controlled product photos plus spectral measurement.

design_traceability_matrix.research_principle: Ultra-low color temperature technologies are relevant to nighttime-friendly light design.
design_traceability_matrix.product_design_decision: Aim for a lower-stimulation warm spectrum direction.
design_traceability_matrix.owlnest_lume_implementation: Deep amber nighttime glow for bedside or room placement.

recommended_wording: designed around published research on evening light exposure and blue-light reduction
supports a more natural evening wind-down
helps create a calmer nighttime environment
designed for the 1 to 2 hours before bed
avoided_wording: proven to make you sleep better

product_validation_roadmap.current_status: Product design is evidence-informed. Product-specific laboratory validation should be added when available.
recommended_next_steps: illuminance / lux at common bedside distances
blue-light percentage or melanopic equivalent daylight illuminance if available
photos of actual product under controlled lighting
third-party lab or instrument report if available
